summary: InkDrop backend + frontend e2e tested. Backend: 18/18 pytest passed (auth, story generate/continue/share/export/delete, characters CRUD, community feed, daily prompt, voice auth gate). Frontend: landing, auth, dashboard, compose, vibe selection, generation (~275 words, no banned words), reader controls, share->community, library, logout, protected-route redirect all working. Two minor issues found on Compose/Dashboard around character locker UI refresh and one timing-related daily-prompt-text visibility check.

minor.issue: Pydantic EmailStr rejects .test TLD (returns 422). Documented requirement in task used .test domain. Consider relaxing or document for users. Not a bug per se, but spec mismatch.

ui_bugs.issue: After clicking [data-testid=char-save], no [data-testid^=char-card-] element appears within 1.5s on the Compose page (character list not refreshed in UI even though POST /api/characters succeeds in network)
Newly created character (e.g. 'Mira') not visible in Dashboard Lore Locker text after navigating back. Dashboard likely not re-fetching characters on focus/mount.

[data-testid=daily-prompt-text] was not immediately visible right after login (returned None on query), though [data-testid=daily-prompt-write] button rendered. Possibly mounted after async fetch with no skeleton/testid on the text node; consider keeping the testid on the wrapper.

action_items: Compose page: after POST /api/characters, append the returned character to the local list / refetch so the [data-testid=char-card-{id}] grid updates without page reload.
Dashboard: refetch /api/characters on mount/focus so Lore Locker reflects characters created elsewhere (e.g. in Compose).
Dashboard daily-prompt: ensure [data-testid=daily-prompt-text] is on the always-rendered wrapper (placeholder/skeleton) so it is queryable even while loading.
(Optional) Either accept .test domain in EmailStr or update product copy/tests to use a real TLD; backend currently 422s for *.test.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~580 lines and mixes auth, story, characters, voice, prompts — consider splitting into routers per resource (auth_router.py, story_router.py, characters_router.py) before it grows further.

story_get and story_export both fetch with $or {user_id, is_public}. Currently any authenticated user can fetch ANY public story's full text and even export it. That seems intended for community, but make sure tests/UI consistently treat 'public' stories as read-only for non-owners (cannot continue/delete — already enforced).
JWT_SECRET is committed in /app/backend/.env. Acceptable for preview but flag for production rotation.
Word-count rule in prompt says 250–350 but no server-side enforcement; LLM occasionally drifts. Consider truncation/regeneration if out of bounds (observed 275 words this run — fine).

success_rate.frontend: ~90% (core flows pass; 2 minor character-locker refresh bugs)

test_credentials: fe-1781165404@example.com / TestPass123! (created during FE run). E2E backend tests self-create users with @example.com.
seed_data_creation: Test users + 1 shared story + 1 character left in DB from the run; safe to ignore or purge.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend stable. When retesting frontend, focus on (1) character creation -> immediate appearance in Compose char grid via [data-testid^=char-card-], (2) Dashboard Lore Locker refresh after character add, (3) Reader Continue + Co-Author flows (not exercised in this iteration to save time, but selectors present and backend verified). Use @example.com emails (.test is rejected by Pydantic EmailStr).
rca of the issue: Character locker refresh: Compose.jsx likely posts to /api/characters but does not setState/refetch the local characters list after success. Dashboard.jsx Lore Locker likely uses a one-shot useEffect on mount without re-fetch on route focus. Both are pure frontend state-sync bugs; backend POST + GET /api/characters verified working via pytest.
